settings.title: A New Restorative Class for Tired Evenings
settings.excerpt: Our Wednesday evening slow flow is now a fully restorative class, built for unwinding rather than working out.
settings.body:
Not every practice needs to leave you sweaty. We have reshaped our Wednesday evening class at Stillpoint into a properly restorative session, held with bolsters, blankets, and very few poses.

It used to be a slow flow, which many of you loved, but we kept hearing that what people really wanted by Wednesday evening was to do less, not a gentler version of more. So we listened.

What the class is like

Each shape is held for several quiet minutes, supported so your body can fully let go. There is no pushing, no flowing, just slow breath and gentle release at the end of a long day.

A sense of what to expect:

  • Only a handful of poses across the whole hour.
  • Plenty of props, so your body is held rather than working.
  • Long, quiet holds with time simply to rest.

Who it is for

It suits everyone, including complete beginners and anyone carrying tension or stiffness. You will likely sleep better that night, which several regulars have already told us is their main reason for coming.

Mats and props are provided. Come straight from work if you need to, no special preparation required. Wear something warm and comfortable, as you cool down when you are still for that long.
